reasoning: As of May 13, 2025, Berkeley Earth predicted an 18% chance that 2025 will be the warmest year on record. Furthermore, reports indicate that temperatures have remained stubbornly high despite a recently ended La Niña, preventing a steep drop-off after the record-shattering 2024. However, the immense margin by which 2024 broke records, combined with the transition to ENSO-neutral/La Niña conditions for much of the first half of 2025, makes it difficult for any single year to immediately top 2024 — which is why the probability is relatively low. The Polymarket price was at 10% in mid-June. Because Berkeley Earth calculates an 18% objective probability reflecting stubborn near-term warmth, an estimate of 15% appropriately splits the difference between the scientific model and the market skepticism.
